The potential depends only on \(\theta\), so Laplace’s equation in spherical coordinates reduces to a form whose solution involves \(\ln(\tan(\theta/2))\).  
The capacitance is derived from the total charge on the cone, leading to an expression with \(\ln(\tan(\theta/2))\) in the denominator.  
Thus, the correct answer is option C.

\boxed{C}
